mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces market participants to price in tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns dominate when CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding five percent confirms structural labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized monthly decline in housing starts and retail sales indicates a broad-based collapse in aggregate demand that directly reduces near-term corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint monthly declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative returns with a Rank IC above 0.01, or yield positive returns in the majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle signals improving factory utilization and corporate earnings momentum, which triggers positive short-term S&P 500 returns as investors price in economic stabilization.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles produce negative or flat SPX 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate falling below two percent signals aggressive monetary accommodation that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in greater than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in total nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate confirms deepening labor market weakness that suppresses consumer demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS and UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC above 0.02, or yields positive returns in the majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real gross domestic product reflects weakening aggregate demand that directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and profit growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines are followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing above its six-month moving average signals a supply-side rebound and improving economic momentum, which triggers positive short-term S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate earnings recovery.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of instances when INDPRO crosses above its 6-month moving average.
